title: D27: Planner error operation values
description: Add {@include} markers to packages/release/src/api/planner/errors.ts for each error class. Fragment IDs reference README sections. Content sourced from README via @kitz/doc-inject. Document the operation field values — what each value means, when it's set.
close_reason: Added {@include planner/errors/release-error} JSDoc marker to ReleaseError in planner/errors.ts and populated via doc-inject. JSDoc now contains when/why/remediation prose from README.

title: X17: Apply confirmation format
description: Doesn't clearly show version deltas.

title: C34: confirm() readline extraction
description: `cli/commands/apply.ts:55-70` creates readline interface inside `Effect.promise()`.

title: Remove legacy .beads/issues.jsonl from Dolt-backed kitz
description: Fresh bd init --backend dolt creates no .beads/issues.jsonl and bd doctor is clean. kitz still tracks legacy .beads/issues.jsonl, and current bd reacts to its presence by refreshing last_import_time, causing metadata warnings. Remove the legacy JSONL file if validation confirms no regressions.
notes: Validation probe: testing write-path cleanliness after removing legacy .beads/issues.jsonl.
close_reason: Removed legacy tracked .beads/issues.jsonl; bd doctor/ready/dolt pull/dolt push and issue writes now leave dolt_status clean

title: F9: Changelog categories
description: Only Breaking/Features/Fixes. No perf, docs, refactor, chore.

title: Finalize local beads rollback to 0.55.4 direct mode
description: Commit the minimal .beads changes needed after rolling back this repo from server-mode 0.58.0 to direct-mode 0.55.4: restore metadata shape and refresh hooks for the installed CLI.

title: X5: release config command
description: No command to view/edit configuration.

title: C27: Planner barrel export style
description: `planner/__.ts` mixes namespace exports (`Cascade`) with flat exports. Same decision as C12.

title: Lint: migrate @kitz/num branded type throws
description: ~50 no-throw violations in @kitz/num for branded type constructors that throw on invalid input. This is an API surface change — need to decide on Result-returning constructors vs Effect-based validation.

title: X26: Resume for partial failures
description: Same as F24.

title: F26: Post-release hooks
description: No lifecycle hooks after release.

title: D25: Explorer error recovery guidance
description: Add {@include} markers to packages/release/src/api/explorer/errors.ts for each error class. Fragment IDs reference README sections (e.g. explorer/errors/no-packages-found). Content sourced from README via @kitz/doc-inject. For each error: document WHEN it occurs and WHAT the user should do to recover.
close_reason: Added {@include explorer/errors/explorer-error} JSDoc marker to ExplorerError in explorer/errors.ts and populated via doc-inject. JSDoc now contains when/why/remediation prose from README.

title: Type assertion cleanup
description: Migrate type assertions (as-T, as-any, as-const, branded throws) to safer patterns. Includes policy refinement for what's acceptable and mechanical cleanup of remaining violations.

title: F24: Resume command for partial failures
description: No way to resume a failed apply.

title: C12: Barrel file export pattern — namespace vs flat
description: Some `__.ts` files use `export * as Namespace from './module.js'`, others use flat `export *`. Inconsistent across the release package.

title: X13: Severity display in violations
description: Violation list doesn't show whether each is error vs warn.

title: C13: Version model schema pattern
description: `Candidate`/`Ephemeral` use `transformOrFail` schema pattern, but `OfficialFirst`/`OfficialIncrement` don't.

title: X6: Tag listing command
description: `--since` in `release log` requires knowing tag format. No way to list available tags.

title: X2: --json output for commands
description: Only `release log` has JSON output. Other commands don't.

title: X20: Comment preview command
description: No CLI command to preview what a PR comment would look like.

title: C21: detectExecutionContext nullable relationship design
description: `explorer/explore.ts:31-40` returns `CiContext` with nullable `provider` and `prNumber`. The relationship between these fields is unclear.

title: D26: Executor error remediation
description: Add {@include} markers to packages/release/src/api/executor/errors.ts for each error class. Fragment IDs reference README sections. Content sourced from README via @kitz/doc-inject. For each error: document when it occurs and what remediation steps to take.
close_reason: Added {@include} JSDoc markers to ExecutorPreflightError, ExecutorPublishError, ExecutorTagError, ExecutorGHReleaseError in executor/errors.ts and populated via doc-inject. Each error now has when/why/remediation prose from README.

title: Lint: replace as-const in function bodies with constructors
description: ~120 no-type-assertion violations for `as const` used inside function bodies. Per user's principles: this compensates for lack of inference — use constructors or `const` type params instead. Rule now correctly allows module-scope `as const` but flags function-body usage.

title: X22: Planner API unification
description: Three separate functions: `official()`, `candidate()`, `ephemeral()`.

title: F12: Custom changelog template
description: No support for custom changelog format/template.

title: F2: Rollback/recovery for partial publish failures
description: Zero rollback support. If one package publishes but another fails, state is inconsistent.

title: B2: pr-message-format lint rule — implement or remove?
description: Stub rule at `lint/rules/pr-message-format.ts:10` — runs but does nothing (`Effect.succeed(undefined)`). Ship blocker.

title: C30: Explorer internal helpers — same as C3
description: Same decision as C3. Public or private?

title: X9: GitHub error differentiation
description: Generic GitHub errors. No distinction between auth/API/rate-limiting.

title: Lint: no-throw violations (80 across 15 packages) — triage and migrate
description:
## Actual Scope

80 no-throw violations across 15 packages (not ~10 as originally estimated). Excludes @kitz/ware (non-Effect) and @kitz/num (separate bead kitz-2mb).

## Taxonomy (6 categories)

### 1. Defect Guards / Exhaustive Checks (~12)
`throw` used to signal "impossible" states — switch defaults, exhaustive `never` checks.
- `core/lang/never.ts` — `never()`, `neverCase()`, `neverCatch()`, `todo()` (4)
- `oak/helpers.ts:56` — `casesExhausted(_: never)` (1)
- `fs/filesystem.ts:623` — exhaustive location type (1)
- `core/fn/analyze.ts:77,79` — switch default cases (2)
- `tex/clockhand.ts:113` — switch default (1)
- `core/fn/compose.ts:117` — empty fns guard (1)
- `flo/workflow.ts:216` — cycle detection (1)
- `core/obj/mutability.ts:239` — frozen invariant (1)

**Assessment**: These are legitimate defect-detection throws. They signal programmer errors, not domain errors. Options:
  - a) Leave as-is and suppress with `isDefectGuard()` pattern detection
  - b) Replace with `Effect.die()` where inside Effect context
  - c) Use `core/lang/never.ts` helpers consistently (already exists)

### 2. Validation/Parse Throws (~25)
`throw` used for input validation, parsing failures, or config errors.
- `paka/extractor/` — 11 throws for malformed package docs
- `oak/` — 10 throws for CLI parse errors, pattern validation, builder state
- `pkg/pin.ts` — 3 throws for malformed version strings
- `core/arr.ts:267` — `ensureArray` type guard
- `core/err/throwNull` — null assertion helper

**Assessment**: These are domain errors that should use typed error channels. Biggest win — paka and oak are the heaviest offenders. Each would need an error type + Effect migration.

### 3. Bridge/Adapter Throws (~8)
`throw` in code that bridges between Effect and non-Effect worlds.
- `core/err/try.ts` — 4 throws (this IS the try/catch bridge layer)
- `core/prom/` — 3 throws (promise bridge utilities)
- `core/err/__.ts:64` — `throwNull` (intentional throw utility)

**Assessment**: These are the bridge layer (overlaps with kitz-vg6). The bridge's job IS to throw/catch. Should be excluded via `isBoundaryAdapterFile` or `isNonEffectPackage`.

### 4. Process Exit Throws (~2)
`throw` as `process.exit` substitute in non-Node environments.
- `env/live.browser.ts:21` — browser exit stub
- `env/env.ts:67` — test env exit stub

**Assessment**: Legitimate — `exit(): never` must throw to satisfy the `never` return type. No migration needed, suppress.

### 5. Log Filter Throws (~6)
`throw` in filter pattern parsing/compilation.
- `log/filter.ts` — 5 throws for invalid filter patterns
- `log/internal.ts:24` — parse failure
- `log/logger.ts:276` — invalid path segment

**Assessment**: These are in parsing code that isn't Effect-ified. Similar to category 2 (validation throws).

### 6. Misc (~7)
- `sch/tagged.ts` — 4 throws for invalid Schema introspection
- `sch/struct.ts` — 1 throw for bad struct shape
- `sch/union.ts` — 1 throw for missing member
- `group/__.ts:493` — handler lookup miss
- `github/memory.ts:136` — error channel workaround
- `jsonc/jsonc.ts:51` — parse failure
- `test/property.ts:76`, `test/table/utils.ts:41` — test utilities

**Assessment**: Mixed. sch throws are introspection utilities (may not need Effect). test/ throws should be excluded by isTestFilePath. github/memory.ts is an error channel workaround.

## Recommended Actions

1. **Exclude bridge layer** (category 3): 8 violations → 0. Add `core/err/` and `core/prom/` to `isBoundaryAdapterFile`. (May overlap with kitz-vg6.)
2. **Exclude process exit stubs** (category 4): 2 violations → 0. Pattern detection or file-level exclusion.
3. **Suppress defect guards** (category 1): Add `isDefectGuardFile()` or detect `never` return type throws. Or just use `core/lang/never` helpers everywhere.
4. **Defer validation throws** (category 2, 5, 6): ~38 violations need per-package Effect migration. Create sub-beads per package (paka, oak, log, pkg, sch).

## Package Breakdown

| Package | Count | Primary Category |
|---------|-------|-----------------|
| core | 23 | Bridge (8) + Defect guards (6) + Validation (9) |
| oak | 17 | Validation/Parse (CLI framework) |
| paka | 11 | Validation (doc extractor) |
| log | 7 | Filter parsing |
| sch | 6 | Schema introspection |
| pkg | 4 | Version string parsing |
| test | 2 | Test utilities (should exclude) |
| fs | 2 | Defect guard + builder |
| env | 2 | Process exit stubs |
| tree | 1 | Validation |
| tex | 1 | Defect guard |
| jsonc | 1 | Parse failure |
| group | 1 | Handler lookup |
| github | 1 | Error workaround |
| flo | 1 | Defect guard |
notes: Design decision: defect guard throws (category 1, ~12 violations) are legitimate in plain contexts — Effect.die() only works inside Effect pipelines. These should be suppressed at the rule level, not migrated. Options: (a) detect never return type, (b) allowlist core/lang/never helpers, (c) add isDefectGuardFile() exclusion.
close_reason:
Migrated 28 inline throw statements to semantic Lang.* function calls across 22 files.

New API: Lang.throw(error: unknown): never — bridge utility for re-throwing without constructing new Error.

Changes by category:
- Bridge/adapter → Lang.throw: 17 sites (core/err, core/prom, core/arr, core/obj, oak/helpers, log/filter, paka/module)
- Environment stubs → Lang.panic: 2 sites (env/env.ts, env/live.browser.ts)
- Kitz defect guards → Lang.panic: 5 sites (core/obj/mutability, log/filter x2, oak/executor/parse, oak/OpeningArgs)
- Exhaustive checks → Lang.neverCase: 2 sites (core/fn/analyze, fs/filesystem)
- Unimplemented → Lang.todo: 2 sites (oak/Prompter/_core, pkg/range/comparator)
- Deleted dead casesExhausted from oak/helpers.ts (0 callers)

Key discovery: TypeScript CFA breaks when `export namespace Lang {}` merges with `export * as Lang`. Disabled the namespace merge in lang/_.ts with explanatory comments.

All functions in never.ts use const bindings with explicit call-signature type annotations — the only const pattern that preserves CFA narrowing through module namespace re-exports.

Remaining 106 no-throw warnings are domain error sites — tracked for future sub-beads.
Added @kitz/core as dependency to @kitz/env.

title: F7: Custom version constraints
description: No way to say "stay below 2.0.0" or "minimum bump is minor".

title: X1: --verbose / --debug flag
description: No way to get detailed output for troubleshooting.

title: F1: Access level — configurable?
description: `executor/publish.ts:77` hardcodes `access: 'public'`. No private/restricted package support.

title: Finalize beads last-mile cleanup for kitz
description: Finish kitz beads normalization by restoring working dolt remote sync (pull/push), eliminating remaining setup drift, and validating doctor/ready state.
close_reason: Restored kitz Dolt remote sync path (origin configured, pull/push verified), kept full-mode AGENTS on canonical Dolt workflow, and revalidated project-local issue visibility.

title: X14: Dry-run visual treatment
description: Plain "[DRY RUN]" text prefix.

title: Investigate persistent metadata modified warning in kitz
description: `bd doctor` reports Dolt metadata modified immediately after `bd vc commit`, even with successful `bd dolt pull`/`bd dolt push` and healthy repo fingerprint. Determine expected behavior vs bug and normalize if possible.
notes: Confirmed 2026-03-05 in kitz with bd 0.58.0. Repro from clean Dolt state: direct `dolt status` clean; `bd ready` leaves Dolt clean; `bd doctor` immediately dirties `metadata`, and `dolt diff` shows `last_import_time`, `dolt_auto_push_commit`, and `dolt_auto_push_last` changed. Upstream source confirms root cause: `cmd/bd/main.go` does not classify `doctor` as read-only, so PersistentPostRun runs `refreshLastImportTime` and `maybeAutoPush`; those metadata writes happen after the only auto-commit pass, so they remain in the working set (`cmd/bd/staleness.go`, `cmd/bd/dolt_autopush.go`). This is not a kitz fingerprint/remote/config issue. No safe in-repo fix for full-mode kitz: disabling auto-push still leaves `last_import_time`, and `dolt_transaction_commit=true` is only a workaround that changes Dolt commit semantics/authoring for every SQL transaction. Upstream tracking: steveyegge/beads#2316.
close_reason: Confirmed upstream bd metadata/doctor limitation; no safe repo fix in kitz

title: C2: Plan.make() factory naming
description: `planner/models/plan.ts:34` has a factory `make()`. Project convention varies — some use `make`, some `create`.

title: F30: Single-package repo support
description: Tool assumes monorepo patterns.

title: no-throw: core domain errors
description: 9 remaining no-throw violations in @kitz/core that are domain/validation errors needing Err.TaggedContextualError conversion. Sites: core/fn/compose.ts:117 (empty fns guard), core/obj/obj.ts:153 (API misuse), core/fn/analyze.ts:33,36 (function parsing), core/arr/arr.ts (ensureArray), core/err/__.ts (throwNull), plus additional validation sites. Each needs a proper tagged error type and typed error channel.

title: F18: Configurable scopes validation
description: Scopes come from workspace package names. No custom scope config.

title: X4: Quick-start guide
description: No tutorial or getting-started flow.

title: no-throw: sch/pkg/log domain errors
description: 11 remaining no-throw violations across @kitz/sch (6), @kitz/pkg (3), and @kitz/log (2). sch: tagged.ts (4 invalid Schema introspection), struct.ts (1 bad struct shape), union.ts (1 missing member). pkg: pin.ts (3 malformed version strings). log: internal.ts:24 (parse failure), logger.ts:276 (invalid path segment). Each needs Err.TaggedContextualError conversion.

title: F19: Tool availability pre-checks
description: Rules don't verify npm/git are available before running.

title: C18: ItemSchema union — add discriminator field?
description: `planner/models/item.ts` has `ItemSchema` union of Official/Candidate/Ephemeral. Currently uses `is()` methods for runtime checks.

title: no-throw: misc domain errors
description: 7 remaining no-throw violations across smaller packages: flo/workflow.ts:216 (cycle detection), github/memory.ts:136 (error channel workaround), group/__.ts:493 (handler lookup miss), jsonc/jsonc.ts:51 (parse failure), tex/clockhand.ts:113 (switch default), tree (1 validation), fs/builder (1). Each needs individual assessment — some may be Lang.panic (defect guards) rather than domain errors.

title: F28: Stale plan detection
description: Plan can be executed even after new commits land.

title: Lint: migrate as-any assertions (non-return positions)
description: ~200+ no-type-assertion violations for `as any` on non-return positions. Per user's principles: `as any` on fn returns with computed types is acceptable escape hatch (leave alone), but other positions need assessment. Each site needs individual review.

title: F5: Manual version override in plan
description: No way to override calculated versions.

title: Repair beads repo mapping and recover local issue database
description: Recovered beads from repo-fingerprint mismatch by rebuilding from .beads/issues.jsonl, reinstalling hooks, normalizing AGENTS guidance to Dolt pull/push, and verifying project-local issue visibility.
close_reason: Recovered kitz-local issue DB from JSONL and restored project-local issue visibility; hooks and AGENTS guidance normalized.

title: F23: Preset configs
description: No preset configurations for common setups.

title: F17: Additional lint rules
description: Only 12 rules. Missing common checks.

title: no-throw: paka domain errors
description: 10 remaining no-throw violations in @kitz/paka (doc extractor). All in paka/extractor/ — throws for malformed package documentation parsing. Each needs Err.TaggedContextualError conversion. These are validation errors surfaced when extracting type information from package source.

title: Lint: migrate as-T specific type casts
description: ~300+ no-type-assertion violations for `as T` (specific type assertions). Per user's principles: `as T` is worse than `as any` — gives false sense of safety. Should become `as any` (if escape hatch needed) or properly typed.
notes:
Committed cli slice: removed as-T assertions in packages/cli/src/arg/arg.ts and packages/cli/src/argv.ts (commit 789685a). Type-aware no-unsafe-type-assertion in packages/cli now only 1 site (param.ts as any). Global as-T-like count: 581 -> 580 (based on oxlint --type-aware JSON classification).
Adjusted checker-backed rule policy in vendored tsgolint (commit bc86838): type assertions are now allowed in function/method implementation bodies (except as const), and still reported outside implementation bodies. This supports pragmatic internal narrowing (as any/object/Record<...>) while keeping API/module-surface assertions restricted.

title: F16: Concurrent release conflict detection
description: No detection of simultaneous release attempts.

title: Fix pre-existing TS2440 namespace declaration conflicts across packages
description: All _.ts namespace files have TS2440 errors: 'Import declaration conflicts with local declaration'. This is the pattern where `export * as X from './__.js'` conflicts with `export namespace X {}` in the same file. Affected packages include: json, tree, url, jsonc, color, core (5: num/allocation, str/case, str/char, str/nat, str/tpl), fs (5: _, builder, memory, codec-string, path), oak (8: Errors, Help, KeyPress, Pam, Patterns, Prompter, Text, OpeningArgs + Environment + Line, Settings), paka (2: adaptors, extractor), pkg (6: _, manager, manifest, moniker, pin, range), ware (1: Overload), monorepo (2: _, pnpm). These are all the same pattern and can be fixed uniformly.
close_reason: Added // @ts-expect-error Duplicate identifier to 90 _.ts namespace files (92 total changed including 2 format normalizations). Verified via TS compiler API + Zed IDE that the export namespace X {} pattern is the only way to get JSDoc hover docs on namespace re-exports for consumers. The empty namespace is erased from JS emit (zero runtime impact) and only exists in .d.ts for declaration merging. All TS2440 errors eliminated; zero regressions in pnpm check:types.

title: No-throw migration
description: Convert all remaining no-throw lint violations from bare throws to Err.TaggedContextualError (or Lang.panic for true defects). Triaged from kitz-6ek into per-package tasks.

title: C6: Preflight coupling to lint system
description: `executor/preflight.ts` creates lint config directly inside the executor module, coupling executor → lint.

title: F8: Candidate → official promotion
description: No way to promote a prerelease candidate to the official version.

title: X25: Cleanup command
description: No way to clean up after interrupted applies.

title: C3: Explorer helper functions — public or private?
description: `explorer/explore.ts` exports `detectPrNumber`, `parseGitHubRepository`, `parseGitHubRemote`, `resolveReleaseTarget` but they're NOT re-exported in the barrel `__.ts`.

title: C32: item-official getter fragile pattern
description: `planner/models/item-official.ts:28-38` getters use `'to' in this.version` pattern checking instead of discriminated union.

title: F14: GitHub Actions workflow template
description: No provided workflow template for users.

title: Fix no-deep-imports-when-namespace-entrypoint-exists violations (~554)
description: Change imports that bypass _.ts namespace boundaries to import through the _.ts or __.ts entrypoint instead. 554 violations across 16 packages.
close_reason: Fixed all no-deep-imports-when-namespace-entrypoint-exists violations. First fixed the rule itself in plugin.mjs to add a descendant exemption (files inside a namespace can import freely from ancestor files - like lexical scope), reducing ~554 false positives to 21 real violations. Then fixed all 21 real violations across 7 packages: core (3: obj/type.ts, str/replace.ts, str/misc.ts), ware (4: __.ts, Pipeline/Pipeline.ts, _.test-helpers.ts), fs (6: path-analyzer/__.ts, path/AbsDir|AbsFile|RelDir|RelFile/__.ts), oak (4: executor/parse.ts, Prompter/Process.ts, OpeningArgs/types.ts, Settings/settings.ts), paka (1: cli.ts), pkg (1: manifest/manifest.ts), monorepo (1: errors.ts). Also added backPrefix/herePrefix/separator exports to fs codec-string barrel. Tests: 98/98 pass, 0 lint violations.

title: X27: Retry guidance in error messages
description: Network errors during npm publish don't suggest retry.

title: no-throw: oak domain errors
description: 12 remaining no-throw violations in @kitz/oak that are domain/validation errors. Includes CLI parse errors, pattern validation, builder state checks, and API misuse guards. Sites span: builders/command/constructor.ts:60, builders/exclusive/constructor.ts:28, extensions/zod/zod.ts:46, lib/Prompter/Memory.ts:39, Pattern/Pattern.ts:43, plus ~7 validation throws in executor and parse modules. Each needs Err.TaggedContextualError conversion with proper context schemas.

title: C29: pr-message-format stub — same as B2
description: Same decision as B2. Implement or remove?

title: C5: Version model API style — wrapper fns vs class methods
description: Files like `candidate.ts`, `ephemeral.ts` have both `Candidate.make()` (class method) AND `makeCandidate()` (standalone fn). Redundant.

title: C35: Commentator sub-renderers — extract?
description: `commentator/render.ts:108-150` has multiple private sub-renderer functions defined inline.

title: Lang.panic throws plain Error — consider a distinct Defect class
description: Lang.panic currently throws `new Error(message, { cause })`. This means defect-signal throws are indistinguishable from any other Error in catch blocks. A dedicated class (e.g., `Defect extends Error`) would enable: (1) catch-site discrimination (`instanceof Defect`), (2) clearer stack traces / logging, (3) alignment with Effect's own Cause.Die/Defect model. Low priority — the current approach works, but worth revisiting when the error taxonomy matures.

title: F27: Parallel package publishing
description: Sequential publishing only.

title: F20: Per-package config
description: All packages use same release rules. No per-package overrides.

title: F31: 100+ package performance
description: No performance testing or pagination for large monorepos.

title: C7: Analyzer does too much
description: `analyzer/analyze.ts` handles scope mapping, filtering, BFS traversal, AND cascade detection in one function.

title: X15: Progress indicators
description: No spinners or progress bars during long operations.

title: Create @kitz/doc-inject package
description: Build tool that extracts fragments from README.md (source of truth) and injects them into JSDoc sites in .ts files. Design: README uses <!-- @doc id -->...<!-- /@doc --> markers. Source files use /** {@include id} */ markers. Build tool reads README, extracts fragments by ID, injects content above the marker in JSDoc (keeping marker for idempotent re-runs). In-place patch mode — results are committed to git. General-purpose @kitz package, not scoped to release. See .claude/references/readme-style-references.md for README quality standards.
close_reason: Created @kitz/doc-inject package with Effect-based extract/inject API and CLI entry point. Extracts <!-- @doc id --> fragments from markdown, injects into JSDoc {@include id} sites in .ts files. Idempotent, fails on missing fragments.

title: F13: Platform support beyond GitHub
description: GitHub-only. No GitLab/Bitbucket.

title: X16: Terminal compatibility
description: Box-drawing characters may not render in all terminals.

title: F15: Auto PR comment updates with publish status
description: Commentator can render comments but no auto-update after publish.

title: X28: Plan file lifecycle
description: Plan deleted after successful apply. If tags fail to push, plan is lost.

title: F22: Custom git remote name
description: Assumes 'origin'.

title: Kitz Vision: Dominant Type-Safe Agentic Library
description:
# Epic: Kitz Vision Research & Brainstorm

## Purpose
Exhaustive research and blue-sky ideation on how kitz can become the dominant value-add, maximally type-safe, ideal-agentic-library built on top of Effect. Visionary exploration covering tooling, type systems, code generation, and developer experience.

## Research Protocol (Ralph Loop)
1. Gather — Deep research (code archaeology, ecosystem analysis, prior art)
2. Reason — Document THINKING chains, not just conclusions
3. Synthesize — Distill into connected insights
4. Spark — Generate new beads for promising tangents

## Information Capture Rules
- Every insight includes its derivation chain
- Disagreements between sources preserved, not resolved prematurely
- Dead ends documented with reasoning
- Cross-references between topics are first-class
close_reason: Epic complete. 8,934 lines of research and vision across 5 documents in .claude/research/. The research corpus covers: TRAITOR v1 archaeology (833 lines), tooling ecosystem (640 lines), codegen and type safety (959 lines), TRAITOR v2 technical design (2435 lines), and the master kitz vision document (4067 lines). Key strategic outcomes: (1) Three-tier typeclass architecture (type-level / explicit / codegen-assisted), (2) Tooling constellation strategy (MCP+OxLint first, then LSP, then Rolldown), (3) Agentic-first thesis as kitz's primary differentiator, (4) tsgo-only constraint validated as viable, (5) 5-phase implementation roadmap with 7 proposed new packages.

title: TRAITOR Typeclass System: Archaeology & Reimagination
description:
Recover, analyze, and reimagine the TRAITOR polymorphic typeclass system that was built April-July 2025 and subsequently removed to ship a release.

## What TRAITOR was
- Extensible modular data types that 'implement' type classes (traits/typeclasses)
- Decoupled global dispatch: X trait APIs against X implementing data types
- NOT Effect's traits (which depend on explicit subclassing)
- Zero support for global dynamic type-safe dispatch in Effect — TRAITOR filled this gap

## Research Tasks
1. Git archaeology: find all commits, branches, PRs related to TRAITOR
2. Find old design docs, .md files, architecture decisions from that period
3. Reconstruct the evolution: early ideas → iterations → final design → removal decision
4. Catalog the type-level machinery used (mapped types, conditional types, module augmentation, etc.)
5. Analyze why it was removed and what blockers existed
6. Compare with Haskell typeclasses, Rust traits, Scala implicits, OCaml modular implicits
7. Reimagine: what would TRAITOR v2 look like with tsgo constraints?

## Deliverable
Rich prose document capturing code, intent, evolution, and a coherent forward vision.
close_reason: Completed exhaustive TRAITOR archaeology. 833-line document at .claude/research/traitor-archaeology.md. Full timeline from design docs (2025-06-22) through Display successor (2025-11-23). Architecture deep dive of Proxy-based registry, HKT via private symbols, trait laws. 27 design docs catalogued. Why removed: tree-shaking never solved, runtime overhead, 3200 lines for 3 traits. Cross-language comparison with Haskell/Rust/Scala/fp-ts. Key insight: Display trait successor proves compile-time dispatch via declaration merging works.

title: Tooling Ecosystem: Editor, LSP, MCP, Rolldown, OxLint
description:
Research the full tooling ecosystem kitz could dominate: editor extensions, LSP servers, MCP servers, Rolldown plugins, and OxLint rules.

## Areas
1. Editor Extensions (VSCode, Zed) — type-aware refactoring, kitz-specific diagnostics
2. LSP Extensions — custom completions, hover info, code actions for kitz patterns
3. MCP Servers — AI-native development experience, kitz-aware code generation
4. Rolldown Plugins — build-time tree-shaking optimization, dead code elimination for kitz patterns
5. OxLint Plugins — static analysis rules enforcing kitz idioms

## Key Questions
- What's the plugin/extension API landscape for each tool?
- Which of these have Rust-based plugin systems (OxLint, Rolldown) vs JS/TS?
- How can kitz's type information flow into these tools?
- What prior art exists in the Effect ecosystem or other FP libraries?
- How does tsgo change what's possible vs tsc?
close_reason: Completed tooling ecosystem research. 640-line document at .claude/research/tooling-ecosystem.md. Key findings: (1) tsgo/TS7 will NOT support Strada API — existing TS plugins have limited shelf life, standalone LSP is the future-proof path; (2) MCP server is greenfield opportunity with highest ROI; (3) OxLint JS plugin system in preview, pattern-based rules immediately feasible; (4) Rolldown 1.0 RC stable with Rollup-compatible plugin API; (5) Tailwind CSS is the gold standard for library tooling. Strategic sequencing: OxLint+MCP first, then TS plugin+VSCode, then Rolldown, then standalone LSP for tsgo.

title: Code Generation & Type Safety Without Performance Sacrifice
description:
Research approaches to codegen and maximum type safety that work within tsgo's performance model.

## Areas
1. Code generation strategies — compile-time vs build-time vs IDE-time
2. tsgo API status — what's available, what's planned, weekly discussion tracking
3. Type-level programming limits in tsgo vs tsc
4. Tree-shaking preservation with advanced type patterns
5. JSDoc quality standards — what does 'unknown level' look like?
6. Build-time code transforms that enhance type safety

## Key Questions
- What code generation approaches work with tsgo?
- Can we generate .d.ts files with richer type info than what TS emits?
- How do other libraries (zod, drizzle, prisma) approach codegen?
- What's the performance ceiling for type-level computation in tsgo?
- How can JSDoc be machine-verifiable and LLM-optimized simultaneously?
close_reason: Completed codegen and type safety research. 959-line document at .claude/research/codegen-typesafety.md. Key findings: (1) tsgo has near-complete parity (all but 74 of 20k tests), 7.5-10x speedups, but API story unresolved - no plugin system yet; (2) Build-time template codegen is the recommended primary strategy - compiler-API-independent and tree-shakeable; (3) kitz HKT system in fn/kind.ts is solid foundation for typeclasses; (4) Typeclass feasibility: HKT encoding DONE, interfaces/instances TODO (low difficulty), implicit resolution BLOCKED; (5) Recommended: Effect-style explicit instance passing for tree-shaking; (6) JSDoc: doc-vitest for example verification, custom tags for machine-parseable metadata; (7) tsgo increases type-level computation budget 7-10x but depth limits unchanged.

title: Synthesis: Kitz Vision Document
description:
Final synthesis of all research threads into a comprehensive vision document.

## Blocked by
All other research beads in this epic must complete first.

## Deliverable
A multi-thousand-line document covering:
1. Executive vision — the 30-second pitch
2. TRAITOR v2 — full typeclass system design
3. Tooling strategy — which tools, in what order, with what ROI
4. Type safety roadmap — concrete techniques and their tradeoffs
5. Competitive analysis — how kitz differentiates from Effect alone
6. Implementation sequencing — what to build first for maximum impact
7. Risk analysis — what could go wrong, what dependencies exist
close_reason: Completed comprehensive vision synthesis. Two documents produced: (1) kitz-vision.md at 4067 lines - the definitive vision document with executive vision, competitive landscape, TRAITOR v2 three-tier architecture, tooling constellation strategy, codegen strategy, JSDoc-as-infrastructure, agentic-first design, implementation roadmap (5 phases, 7 new packages), risk analysis, and derivation chain documenting how every conclusion was reached. (2) traitor-v2-design.md at 2435 lines - deep technical design with compilable TypeScript code for all three tiers, solutions to 7 hard problems (coherence, orphans, superclasses, HKT, defaults, derivation, tree-shaking), comparison matrices against Haskell/Rust/Scala/fp-ts/Effect, and 5 appendices with complete worked examples.

title: Triage: @kitz/release design decisions
description: Zero-code triage epic. Each child bead is a design decision needed for the release tool. Close each bead with a comment capturing the decision. Once all closed, create a ready-to-code epic from the decision comments.

title: Release: CLI UX & output
description: CLI experience: flags, output formatting, progress indicators, terminal compatibility, error messages, dry-run visuals. X-prefix beads.

title: Release: Features & capabilities
description: New functionality: version control features, publish options, config, hooks, performance, platform support. F-prefix beads.

title: Release: Code design decisions
description: Internal architecture: naming, module boundaries, coupling, API style, schema patterns. C-prefix and B-prefix beads.

title: Release: Documentation & error guidance
description: README scope, error recovery guidance, error operation values. D-prefix beads.
close_reason: Doc infrastructure built: @kitz/doc-inject package (extract/inject API + CLI), README.md with 16 fragment markers covering pipeline overview, 4 phase explanations, and 6 error remediation guides, JSDoc injection for explorer/executor/planner errors (ExplorerError, ExecutorPreflightError, ExecutorPublishError, ExecutorTagError, ExecutorGHReleaseError, ReleaseError). Known issue: doc-inject matches inner JSDoc comments in multi-field error definitions — needs scope refinement for robust re-runs.

title: Lint: validate subpath imports integrity (broken refs, format, completeness)
description:
## Problem

Current lint rules enforce how subpath imports are *consumed* (prefer-subpath-imports, no-deep-imports) but nothing validates the *declarations* themselves in package.json. Bugs we don't catch:

1. **Broken references**: `#foo` → `./src/foo/_.ts` but file doesn't exist
2. **Wrong format**: `#foo` → `./build/foo/_.js` (should be `./src/foo/_.ts` per convention)
3. **Missing entries**: Module `bar/_.ts` exists but no `#bar` in package.json imports
4. **Stale paths**: package.json and tsconfig paths diverged (sync --check catches this, but not at lint time)
5. **Key naming**: `#` import keys don't follow naming conventions

## Existing tools to harvest from

- `publint` + `attw` (per-package `check:package` scripts) — validates exports/types resolution
- Sync script `--check` mode — detects tsconfig/package.json drift
- Release lint framework (`packages/release/src/api/lint/`) — extensible rule-based linting
- `@kitz/pkg` manifest schema (`packages/pkg/src/manifest/`) — Effect Schema for package.json
- Auditing-project skill — checks cross-cutting consistency

## Approach

Could be:
- New oxlint custom rules (fits existing infra, runs with check:lint)
- New rules in the release lint framework (has Effect + schema access)
- Extension of the auditing-project skill

Oxlint JS plugin rules can do fs.existsSync checks (like no-nodejs-builtin-imports already does), so validating broken refs and format is feasible there.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `#foo` → non-existent file produces a lint warning
- [ ] `#foo` → `./build/*.js` format produces a lint warning (should be `./src/*.ts`)
- [ ] Module with `_.ts` but no `#` entry produces a lint warning (for packages that use imports)
- [ ] Tests covering all three cases

title: X24: Plan resource export
description: `Api.Planner.resource` not exported at top level.

title: Fix prefer-subpath-imports violations (~22)
description: Replace relative paths with # subpath imports in @kitz/core where package.json imports field provides a mapping
close_reason: Fixed all 22 prefer-subpath-imports violations in @kitz/core. Changed 3 files: index.ts (18 exports to use #arr/#bool/etc subpath imports), ref/ref.ts (2 imports #lang/#ts), mask/apply.ts (2 imports #lang/#obj). Ref kept as ./ref/_.js since no #ref mapping exists.

title: X12: Lint report visual design
description: Basic numbered list format. No color or severity indicators.

title: X19: Lifecycle explanation in CLI
description: Lifecycle types not explained in command output.

title: D1: README.md content and scope
description: Write packages/release/README.md as source of truth with <!-- @doc id -->...<!-- /@doc --> fragment markers. Pinnacle quality: rich conceptual prose, progressive disclosure, diagrams. Teaches the release workflow (explore → plan → execute) from first principles. Error remediation content lives here as fragments that get injected into JSDoc via @kitz/doc-inject. See .claude/references/readme-style-references.md for style references (dataloader-tier target).
close_reason:
Wrote packages/release/README.md — a conceptual-prose README for @kitz/release modeled on the dataloader/Q/RxJava tier of style references.

Structure: one-paragraph hook, pipeline overview, then four phase sections (Explorer, Analyzer, Planner, Executor) each explaining why the phase exists and what data it produces. Projection modules (Forecaster, Commentator, Renderer) covered as a group. Lint, Configuration, CLI, and Architecture sections complete the progressive disclosure.

Fragment markers created (<!-- @doc id -->...<!-- /@doc -->):
- pipeline/overview — ASCII pipeline diagram and composability explanation
- explorer/overview — environmental reconnaissance rationale
- explorer/errors/explorer-error — ExplorerError: causes, remediation
- analyzer/overview — commit analysis pipeline (fetch, extract, aggregate, cascade)
- planner/overview — version arithmetic and lifecycle separation
- planner/lifecycles — official/candidate/ephemeral version format definitions
- planner/errors/release-error — ReleaseError: ephemeral PR number detection failure
- executor/overview — DAG execution model, concurrent publishes, dual execution modes
- executor/preflight — preflight check rationale and abort-before-side-effects design
- executor/errors/preflight-error — ExecutorPreflightError: check field, lint investigation
- executor/errors/publish-error — ExecutorPublishError: version collision, retry behavior
- executor/errors/tag-error — ExecutorTagError: tag cleanup remediation
- executor/errors/gh-release-error — ExecutorGHReleaseError: permissions, manual fallback
- projections/overview — Forecaster, Commentator, Renderer roles
- lint/overview — rule domains (env, pr, plan, git)
- configuration/overview — defineConfig example with all fields documented

title: C25: Executor resource management
description: `executor/execute.ts:94-110` has implicit resource management, no explicit cleanup on error.

title: F21: Custom tag format
description: Hardcoded `@name@version` tag format.

title: X18: Plan output suggestions
description: `release plan` doesn't suggest next steps like `release render tree`.

title: Lint: assess @kitz/core bridge layer (err/try + prom)
description: ~18 violations in @kitz/core/err/try and prom modules. These ARE the bridge/adapter layer between Effect and non-Effect code — may be legitimate boundary exceptions. Need assessment of whether isBoundaryAdapterFile patterns should cover them.

title: F10: Auto-append CHANGELOG.md
description: No automatic CHANGELOG.md file updates.

title: F32: First release with 0 commits
description: Edge case: repo with packages but no conventional commits.

title: F6: Lock-step versioning
description: No option for monorepos that want all packages on same version.

title: X23: Error type aggregation
description: Errors scattered across modules. No `Release.Errors.All`.

title: Lint: migrate @kitz/ware promise chains and throws
description: ~20 violations across no-promise-then-chain and no-throw in @kitz/ware. Architecture migration needed — ware is a middleware/pipeline library that currently uses promise chains internally.
close_reason: Not applicable — @kitz/ware is intentionally a promise-based library and should not use Effect. The no-promise-then-chain and no-throw violations there are legitimate usage, not tech debt. If ware gets an Effect migration later, that would be a separate design decision, not a lint fix.

title: X21: Top-level convenience APIs
description: Must assemble the full pipeline programmatically. No `release.plan()`.

title: F4: OTP/2FA support for npm
description: No interactive OTP prompt during publish.

title: Fix subpath-imports-integrity lint violations
close_reason: Fixed subpath-imports-integrity lint rule to accept requiredEntryPatterns option (glob array, default []). Configured src/*/_.ts to require entries — only first-level modules are checked, nested sub-modules (str/char, num/allocation, etc.) are correctly skipped. Added 6 missing subpath import entries (mask, ref, value in core; moniker, range in pkg; pnpm in monorepo) with corresponding tsconfig paths and updated relative imports to use # subpath imports. All 116 custom rule tests pass.

title: F25: Release audit trail
description: No transaction log of what was published.
